deviations: Two international environments (international-prod, international-auth) instead of standard pattern
Naming uses hyphenated suffixes (-1) for multiple variants of same env type
No namespace/secrets/sealed.yaml file present (sealed secrets not used)
PVC/PV naming conventions tie directly to env names

sharedFields: All environments use postStart backup setup

replicas.note: Production envs (prod, prod-1, uat) run 2 replicas for HA; test and international envs run 1 replica

image.tag.note: Two distinct image versions: 23.10.24 (old LTS) vs 26.02.27 (newer); prod/test/uat on 23.10.24, others on 26.02.27

resources.requests.cpu.note: CPU requests scale with environment tier: prod gets 8, test gets 0.5, others scaled accordingly

resources.limits.cpu.note: Limits allow burst capacity; international envs capped at 3 (lower than others)

javaHeap.note: Java heap varies significantly: prod gets 12G, test/uat get 4G, test-1/uat-1 get 1G, international get 5G

DOT_MAIL_SMTP_HOST.note: test and uat use ap-southeast-2 (Sydney), all others use us-east-1

DOT_MAIL_SMTP_PORT.note: Old versions use port 25, newer versions (26.02.27) use port 587 (TLS submission)

TOMCAT_REDIS_SESSION_ENABLED.note: Old versions (23.10.24) disable Redis sessions; newer versions (26.02.27) enable them

GLOWROOT_ENABLED.note: Old versions have glowroot in CMS_JAVA_OPTS but not explicit flag; newer versions have explicit flag; test-1/uat-1 have it disabled

healthcheck.path.note: Old versions use /appconfiguration, newer versions use /probes/alive

healthcheck.intervalSeconds.note: Old versions check every 30s, newer versions every 9s (more aggressive)

healthcheck.healthyThresholdCount.note: Old versions require 5 successful checks; newer versions require 2

probe.periodSeconds.liveness.note: Old versions check every 30s; newer versions every 15s

DOT_REINDEX_THREAD_ELASTICSEARCH_BULK_SI.note: Old versions bulk size 5; newer versions bulk size 1

DOT_REINDEX_THREAD_ELASTICSEARCH_BULK_AC.note: Old versions bulk actions 1; newer versions bulk actions 20

ingress.hosts.note: Old versions use version-suffix in hostname (2310); newer versions have primary + alias pattern

tolerations.note: Newer versions have workload-tier tolerations and nodeSelector; old versions do not

nodeSelector.note: Newer versions pin to standard workload tier; old versions unconstrained

anomalies.description: prod-1, test-1, uat-1, international-prod, international-auth have null/missing initialDelaySeconds for liveness probe while prod, test, uat explicitly set to 30. This is inconsistent.
anomalies.recommendation: Standardize initialDelaySeconds across all environments. Current gap may cause early false-negatives on newer versions.

anomalies.description: Old versions (prod, test, uat) include -javaagent:/data/shared/glowroot/glowroot.jar in CMS_JAVA_OPTS, but newer versions do not. Newer versions have separate GLOWROOT_ENABLED flag.
anomalies.recommendation: This reflects intentional migration from agent-based to flag-based glowroot configuration. Acceptable pattern difference.

anomalies.description: prod explicitly sets podManagementPolicy: Parallel, but all other environments omit it (defaults to OrderedReady). This is inconsistent.
anomalies.recommendation: Either explicitly set Parallel for all multi-replica envs (prod, prod-1, uat) or explicitly set OrderedReady for consistency.

anomalies.description: Each environment has two services: a NodePort service for external ingress, and a headless ClusterIP (None) service for peer discovery. All follow this pattern correctly.
anomalies.recommendation: No action needed. Pattern is correct for StatefulSet use.

anomalies.description: international-prod and international-auth are the ONLY environments with WAFv2 ACL annotation (wafv2-acl-arn). No other envs have WAF protection.
anomalies.recommendation: Consider applying WAF to all environments for consistent security posture, or document why international is privileged.

anomalies.description: All environments use soft (preferred) pod anti-affinity. No required anti-affinity or pod disruption budgets (PDB) present. This allows scheduling on same node if necessary.
anomalies.recommendation: Consider adding requiredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution for prod/uat to guarantee availability, or add PodDisruptionBudget resources.

anomalies.description: No sealed secrets present. All secrets provided via AWS Secrets Manager integration only. Single point of failure if AWS secrets access is compromised.
anomalies.recommendation: Current approach is acceptable for AWS-native deployment. Monitor AWS IAM role usage and rotation.

anomalies.description: All environments have DOT_SITE_KEY_DNS_VALIDATION set to false (or missing, implying false). This disables DNS validation for custom domain setup.
anomalies.recommendation: Confirm this is intentional. If using custom domains, DNS validation should be enabled for security.

anomalies.description: New version envs (prod-1, test-1, uat-1, international-*) use aggressive healthchecks (9s interval, 2 thresholds), while old versions use conservative (30s interval, 5 thresholds). This will cause faster churn on newer versions.
anomalies.recommendation: After migration to v26.02.27 across all envs, align healthcheck parameters to new defaults (9s/2 thresholds) or revert to old (30s/5 thresholds) for consistency.

anomalies.description: international-prod statefulset has DOT_SITE_KEY_DNS_VALIDATION defined on line 87 with no value (empty). This is a YAML error.
anomalies.recommendation: Fix the YAML syntax error. Either remove the line or add a value (false recommended).

anomalies.description: Customer has two distinct dotCMS versions in production: 23.10.24_lts (prod, test, uat) and 26.02.27 (prod-1, test-1, uat-1, international-*). This means 5 environments running old LTS while 4 running new version.
anomalies.recommendation: Plan migration path to consolidate on v26.02.27. Staggered approach using the -1 variants is good; accelerate timeline for old versions.
